literature-report-plan.name: R1-computational-reproducibility

metadata.title: Computational Reproducibility in Science and Biomedical ML

literature-report-plan.invocation-prompt:
create literature report on "Computational Reproducibility in Science and Biomedical ML"
Focus: The reproducibility crisis in scientific computing, with emphasis on biomedical/ML code. Issues: Jupyter notebook reproducibility failures, Docker vulnerabilities, unpinned dependencies, lack of experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), data versioning (DVC), code smells, cybersecurity issues. How our repo addresses these with: uv lock files, Docker-per-flow isolation, MLflow experiment tracking, DVC data versioning, pre-commit hooks, Prefect orchestration. Position NEUROVEX as a solution to the reproducibility crisis.
Context: Our repo is a clean-rewrite MLOps platform that explicitly addresses reproducibility. Docker IS the execution model. uv is the ONLY package manager. Every run is tracked in MLflow. This report provides the "why" — the crisis that motivates our architecture.
Key existing refs to read first: - docs/planning/mlflow-robustifying-plan-and-report-for-reproducibility.md - docs/planning/mlops-practices-report.md
EXCLUDED topics (covered in other reports): - Agentic AI (report 5) - FDA/regulatory compliance details (report R2-medical-mlops) - Specific segmentation methods (report R3) - Microscopy acquisition (report R4)
Repo context: /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops KG domains to update: infrastructure, testing, operations Manuscript section: Introduction (reproducibility motivation), Methods (platform architecture)

seed-papers.paper: Pimentel et al. (2019/2021). "A Large-scale Study About Quality and Reproducibility of Jupyter Notebooks." MSR. https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.07333
Heil et al. (2021). "Reproducibility standards for machine learning in the life sciences." Nature Methods 18, 1132-1135. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-021-01256-7
McDougal et al. (2023). "Five pillars of reproducible computational research." Briefings in Bioinformatics. https://doi.org/10.1093/bib/bbad375
Rodriguez-Sanchez et al. (2025). "Reproducible computational experiments with a conversational tool." arXiv. https://arxiv.org/html/2504.10134v1
Pineau et al. (2021). "Improving Reproducibility in Machine Learning Research." JMLR 22(164):1-20. https://jmlr.org/papers/v22/20-303.html
Haibe-Kains et al. (2020). "Transparency and reproducibility in artificial intelligence." Nature 586, E14-E16. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-020-2766-y
Beam et al. (2020). "Challenges to the Reproducibility of Machine Learning Models in Health Care." JAMA 323(4):305-306. https://doi.org/10.1001/jama.2019.20866
Kapoor & Narayanan (2023). "Leakage and the Reproducibility Crisis in ML-based Science." Patterns. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.patter.2023.100804
Botvinik-Nezer et al. (2020). "Variability in the analysis of a single neuroimaging dataset by many teams." Nature 582, 84-88. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-020-2314-9
Gundersen & Kjensmo (2018). "State of the Art: Reproducibility in Artificial Intelligence." AAAI. https://doi.org/10.1609/aaai.v32i1.11503
Vogt (2022). "Neuroscience Data Analysis in the Cloud." Nature Methods 19, 1034. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-022-01613-0
Reprohackathons: promoting reproducibility in bioinformatics through training. https://doi.org/10.12688/f1000research.155524.1

search-domains.domain: Jupyter notebook reproducibility failure rate scientific computing
biomedical research code quality software engineering practices
